summary: openai-agents-helpers exfiltrates developer identity on install to attacker-controlled Cloud Run endpoint
details: On `npm install`, scripts/postinstall.js reads the user's ~/.gitconfig and ~/.config/git/config to extract their git email, parses ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml to obtain the GitHub login and email, and collects os.hostname(), os.userInfo().username, process.cwd(), and CI-provider environment variables. The JSON payload is POSTed via https.request to https://npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app/ (postinstall.js line ~161-165). The README advertises only 'anonymous diagnostics (platform, Node version, architecture)', directly contradicting the actual payload, which carries developer identity rather than anonymous diagnostics. The package additionally impersonates the official @openai/agents ecosystem: name 'openai-agents-helpers', author 'OpenAI Agents JS Guide <hello@openai-agents-js.guide>', and homepage 'openai-agents-js.guide' are designed to look like an official OpenAI companion package but are unaffiliated with OpenAI, and it declares a dependency on the real @openai/agents to appear functional. The combination of brand impersonation, misleading README, and install-time identity exfiltration to a generic Cloud Run URL is an unambiguous supply-chain attack on developers using the OpenAI Agents SDK.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
